demographic_audit_note: Verified 79yo male patient in Atlantis queue. Cross-check of facility face sheet revealed the primary phone number was the patient's disconnected landline; successfully updated Atlantis with son's (POA) mobile number and confirmed Medicare Advantage PPO status.
home_vs_ltc_determination: SW initially confirmed the goal was Home with family support, but pivoted to LTC on Day 14 after the patient's wound debridement required more intensive monitoring than the family could provide.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2 Check-in: SW reports PT is progressing slowly due to phantom limb pain; family is expressing anxiety regarding the upcoming discharge and the level of care required for the surgical site.
v_card_and_flyer_status: Program flyer delivered to patient; V-Card successfully inserted into the son's (POA) smartphone during the Week 1 education session.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: N/A - Pre-discharge pulse call not conducted as the case reached a 'Hard Stop' due to the LTC pivot.
atlantis_final_sync: No first visit date entered; case closed in Atlantis using the 'Neutral_LTC_Closure' status code following the SW's final determination.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit not scheduled; the patient's transition to a long-term care wing at a sister facility rendered the home transition incentives void.
narrative_summary: Patient was admitted for amputation rehabilitation with an initial goal of returning home. The PN conducted a thorough demographic audit and provided education to the family regarding home health expectations. During the maintenance phase, the family's ability to provide wound care became a primary concern, leading the Social Worker to pivot the discharge plan from home to Long-Term Care (LTC). Per the LTC Filter policy, the PN immediately ceased all home care coordination and performed a Neutral_LTC_Closure in Atlantis.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Initial demographic audit identifies outdated contact information in Atlantis.
Social Worker determines the patient requires a level of care inconsistent with home discharge after clinical decline.

format_2_triples.situation: Atlantis record lists a disconnected landline as the primary contact for the amputation patient.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN audited the facility face sheet and updated Atlantis with the son's mobile number.

format_2_triples.situation: Family expresses intense fear of managing the patient's stump care and mobility at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN educated the family on the Day 1 HHA expectations and then escalated the family's panic to the Social Worker.

format_2_triples.situation: SW formally changes the discharge goal to Long-Term Care (LTC) due to safety concerns.
format_2_triples.action_taken: PN performed a Neutral_LTC_Closure in Atlantis and stopped all home transition work.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN notes the family's growing hesitation in the weekly update and waits for the SW to finalize the discharge plan before taking further action.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Adheres to the SW Boundary by allowing the professional staff to manage the discharge destination without interference.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN walks the family through the HHA equipment requirements for the home while simultaneously flagging the family's 'Caregiver Panic' to the SW for a safety intervention.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows Stage 2 education protocols and uses the Boundary Escalation checklist item to ensure the SW is aware of the potential for a failed home transition.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: PN suggests the family look into a specific Skilled Nursing Facility that specializes in amputation care to help them feel better about the LTC transition.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the SW Boundary and Banned Actions by suggesting specific facilities or directing the discharge destination, which is the sole responsibility of the SW.
